title: Close Release Asset Authority and canonical Release Identity
outOfScope: Full public asset hash graph validation reserved for release_asset_integrity_public_check
Release tag publication ordering reserved for release_atomic_publication
Production release publication and external platform settings

sources.reason: Task 1 acceptance and P0-1/P0-2 review mapping.
Documents contradictory v0.5.29 candidate identities and disabled Release Asset Authority.
Current public checker only enters asset authority when releaseEvidenceAuthority is enabled.
Current release metadata lacks the authority field.
Existing identity and public distribution regression coverage.

problemStatement: The public release checker documents Release Assets as the final evidence authority, but v0.5.29 metadata does not enable that path and Tag candidate files contain conflicting source identities.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: authority field enables public Release Asset inspection
conflicting Tag candidate identities fail closed or are superseded only by valid authoritative assets
provenance, digest source, release tag, and exact Tag target resolve to one identity

riskAssessment.reason: The checker decides whether a public release is trustworthy; accepting a mismatched source identity would invalidate the release evidence boundary.

executionDecision.reason: The review supplies the exact failing authority path and the repository contains the checker and regression-test surfaces.

preReviewWarnings: Review focus: ensure candidate evidence cannot silently remain authoritative and all identity fields refer to the exact immutable Tag target.
Generated release-digests.json is refreshed through check_supply_chain.py, not hand-edited.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Public release metadata explicitly enables release-assets-v1 authority.
The checker validates the enabled authority path and fails closed on missing or contradictory identity evidence.
Regression tests cover disabled authority, enabled authority, mismatched candidate identity, and exact Tag target identity.
Release documentation and generated evidence describe one canonical Release Identity without claiming production publication in this Work Item.
guidelines: Treat Release Assets as authoritative only when the explicit release-assets-v1 contract is present and validated.
Do not accept a candidate source identity that differs from the exact immutable Tag target.
Do not claim full public asset integrity; that is the next dependent Work Item.

intent.problem: The public checker documents Release Assets as the final release evidence authority, but the current release metadata does not enable that path and candidate evidence can contain conflicting source identities.
constraints: Keep the Release Asset authority contract explicit and fail closed when required evidence is missing.
Do not claim full asset graph integrity or atomic publication in this Work Item; those are dependent Work Items.
Keep generated release evidence source-bound and do not hand-edit generated baselines as a substitute for the generator.
intent.rationale: Making the authority path executable and testing the canonical identity boundary closes the first P0 trust-model mismatch before adding full public asset integrity validation.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the full PR merge and remediation execution flow.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
